disclaimer: Research summary for family discussion — not medical or legal advice. Verify at official sources.

california.title: Under 16 cannot operate Class 3 e-bike
california.summary: A person under 16 years of age shall not operate a class 3 electric bicycle.

california.title: Under 18 helmet required
california.summary: Riders under 18 must wear an ASTM or CPSC approved helmet on streets and bikeways.

california.title: Lights and reflectors required at night
california.summary: Bicycles operated at night need a white front light, red rear reflector/light, and side reflectors.

huntington_beach.title: Beach/park paths: 10 mph max
huntington_beach.summary: Beach bicycle path and city parks: 10 mph maximum; 5 mph when pedestrians present.

huntington_beach.title: HBPD free e-bike training
huntington_beach.summary: Huntington Beach Police offer free youth e-bike safety training with parent participation.

scooters.title: E-scooter helmet under 18
scooters.summary: California requires helmets for riders under 18 on motorized scooters (same helmet rule as bikes on bikeways).

scooters.title: HB paths & sidewalks
scooters.summary: Slow to 10 mph on beach/park paths; many areas prohibit sidewalk riding — use bike lanes or streets.

3.description: Pedal-assist, motor stops at 28 mph — illegal for under 16
